inputs.text: Main thaka hua mehsoos kar raha hoon, bukhar bhi hai, sar dard hai

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Low-grade fever and malaise are common in viral fever

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Typhoid is less likely at this stage, but cannot be ruled out

inputs.text: Mere bukhar roz badh raha hai, pet dard bhi hai, kabz ho raha hai

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Abdominal pain and constipation are common in gastroenteritis

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Viral fever can cause similar symptoms, but the step-wise increase in fever is more suggestive of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: The step-wise increase in fever is a key feature of typhoid

inputs.text: Mere tez bukhar hai, kabz ke baad dast ho gaya hai, mujhe bahut kamzori mehsoos ho rahi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: The combination of high-grade fever, constipation followed by diarrhea, and severe weakness is highly suggestive of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Gastroenteritis can cause some similar symptoms, but the severity of the symptoms and the presence of constipation followed by diarrhea are more suggestive of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Malaria can cause fever and weakness, but the other symptoms are not typical of malaria

inputs.text: Mujhe bahut thakaan mehsoos ho rahi hai, mere bukhar abhi tak nahin gaya hai, pet mein bahut dard hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: The persistence of high-grade fever, severe weakness, and abdominal pain, along with the presence of petechiae, confirms the diagnosis of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the severity of the symptoms and the presence of petechiae

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the lack of typical symptoms of malaria
